insight:
Yesterday was a solid day! Your 5.1km run at 160 bpm average shows you're maintaining good aerobic intensity without overdoing it just days before Chester. With 2,729 calories burned and 9,230 steps, you were nicely active. That 7.4 hours of sleep and low stress level of 21/100 demonstrate excellent recovery management in this crucial final week.

Your HRV at 112ms sits beautifully in your optimal range, and that 45 bpm resting heart rate confirms you're well-recovered. These numbers give you the green light for today's planned session - your body is responding well to the taper and ready for whatever training you have scheduled.

Since you burned significantly more calories than your 2,300 target yesterday, focus on eating adequately today to support recovery. With only 4 days to go, prioritize getting those 175g of protein to maintain muscle recovery, and don't under-fuel your body when it's working this well.
